(916) 322·5662 
Harlan Curwick 
Administratian 
322·5660 
Stuart B. Scudder 
Assistant City Attorney 
415 Diamond Street 
Redondo Beach, CA 
95804 
Executive/legal 
32~·5901 
Enfarcement 
322-6«1 
November 21, 1983 
Statements af Ecanomic Interest 
322·64« 
Re: 
Advice Letter No. A-83-238 
Dear Messrs. Curwick and Scudder: 
Thank you for your request for advice on the conflict of 
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act. 
The facts, as 
stated in Mr. Scudder's letter and in my telephone conversation 
with Mr. Curwick, are that Mr. Curwick is the Redondo Beach City 
Planning Director. 
Recently, he obtained his real estate 
broker's license and he is considering entering into an 
independent contractor relationship with a real estate office. 
Although he has not determined where he will locate his 
business, he is considering both the Redondo Beach area and the 
Big Bear area, the latter of which is at least 100 miles from 
Redondo Beach. 
He has asked for an explanation of how the 
conflict of interest provisions will apply if he becomes active 
in real estate. 
Government Code Section 871001/ provides that no local 
public official shall make, participate in the making, or in any 
way attempt to use his position to influence,2/ a governmental 
decision in which he/she knows or has reason to know he/she has 
a financial interest. 
An official has a "financial interest" in 
1/ Hereinafter all statutory references are to the 
Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
1/ For the definition of "make, participate in the 
making, or in any way attempting to use his position to 
influence," see the enclosed copy of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 
18700. 


Harlan Curwick 
November 21, 1983 
Page 2 
a decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is 
reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material 
financial effect,lI distinguishable from the effect on the 
public generally,i/ on: 
* * * 
(c) 
Any source of income • • . aggregating $250 
or more in value provided to, received by or promised 
to the public official within 12 months prior to the 
time when the decision was made •••• 
Section 87103(c). 
If Mr. Curwick enters into an independent contractor 
relationship with a real estate agency and begins selling 
property the agency will become a source of income to him. 
In 
addition, if he sells any real property, the buyer, seller, or 
both buyer and seller, may become a source of income to him, 
depending upon whom he represents, once he receives or becomes 
entitled to $250 or more in fees. 
As planning director, Mr. Curwick will be required to 
disqualify himself from making, participating in the making, or 
in any way attempting to use his position to influence a 
decision 57 which will foreseeably have a material financial 
effect on the agency or any buyer or seller who is a source of 
income. 
As I discussed on the telephone with Mr. Curwick, if he 
chooses to limit his business to Big Bear and is not active in 
Redondo Beach, this will, of course, limit the number of 
decisions requiring his disqualification because it is much more 
unlikely that an individual or company buying or selling 
property in Big Bear will be materially affected by planning 
decisions in Redondo Beach. 
11 
For an explanation of the phrase "material financial 
effect," see the enclosed copy 0 f 2 ca 1. Adm. Code Section 
18702. 
!I For an explanation of the "public generally" 
exception, see the enclosed copy of 2 cal. Adm. Code Section 
18703. 
~/ I have included copies of the Harron and Thacher 
advice letters which discuss the phrase "using his position to 
influence." 


Harlan Curwick 
November 21, 1983 
Page 3 
Mr. Curwick must decide whether he is required to disqualify 
himself on a decision-by-decision basis. 
If, on a particular 
decision, he is unable to determine whether disqualification is 
required, he can contact our office for advice at 
(916) 322-5901. 
JSM:plh 
Enclosures 
Very truly yours, 
2~ SIU:uz£?rlZeDII/ 
Janis Shank McLean 
Counsel 
Legal Division 


GORDON C. PHILLIPS 
CITY ATTORNEY 
Robert D. Blaiser, Jr. 
415 DIAMOND STREET 
REDONDO BEACH, CALIFORNIA 90277 
October 18, 1983 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804 
( 
SUBJECT: 
CONFLICT OF INTEREST OF PLANNING DIRECTOR WORKING AS A REAL 
ESTATE BROKER 
Dear Mr. Blaiser: 
TELEPHONE 
(213) 372·1171 
The Planning Director of Redondo Beach has become a licensed real estate 
broker. 
He has requested my assistance in planning his activities to avoid 
the conflict of interest. 
I have located two F.P.P.C. oplnlons that are helpful. 
These are No. 76-005 
(Owen/City of Davis) and No. 75-083 (Oglesby/Antioch). 
However, neither 
deals with a planning director. 
Are you aware of any unpublished advice to 
a planning director? 
Our planning director would restrict his personal brokerage activities to in 
excess of two miles beyond the city boundaries. 
Also, he owns property in 
Big Bear. 
He anticipates using his license in that city. 
I would be 
concerned that agents under his supervision may be active in Redondo Beach or 
within two miles thereof. 
This problem may have been addressed in some oplnlons in your files. 
I would 
appreciate your aid in researching F.P.P.C. files and sending me copies of 
the opinions. 
Cc: 
Curwick 
vp~ 
Stuart B. Scudder 
Assistant City Attorney 


.. 
orIlla 
air Political 
actices Commissioll 
Administration

322·5660 
Stuart B. Scudder 
Assistant City Attorney 
415 Diamond Street 
Redondo Beach, CA 
90277 
Dear Mr. Scudder: 
••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
f ,ecutive/legal 
32?·5901 
Enforcement 
322·6441 
October 24, 1983 
Re: 
A-83-238 
Stotements of Economic Interest 
322·6444 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political 
Reform Act has been referred to Janis Shank McLean, an 
attorney in the Legal Division of the Fair Political 
Practices Commission. 
If you have any questions about your 
advice request, you may contact this attorney directly at 
(916) 322-5901. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex 
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to 
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21 
working days. 
BAM:plh 
Very truly yours, 
A{,{U!ltlf tL {/ YJtd~u 
Barbara A. Milman 
Chief, Legal Division

sections.question_synthetic: What conflict of interest provisions under the Political Reform Act apply to a city planning director who obtains a real estate broker's license and considers working as an independent contractor for a real estate office, potentially in either Redondo Beach or Big Bear?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: As planning director, Mr. Curwick must disqualify himself from making, participating in, or attempting to influence governmental decisions that will foreseeably have a material financial effect on his real estate agency, clients, or other sources of income aggregating $250 or more. If he limits his business to Big Bear rather than Redondo Beach, this will reduce the number of decisions requiring disqualification, and he can seek FPPC advice on a decision-by-decision basis if uncertain.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains incoming request letter (October 18) and administrative acknowledgment (October 24) followed by the FPPC's formal advice letter (November 21, 1983). The main advice letter lacks explicit QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers but contains a clear factual statement and legal analysis. Minor OCR artifacts present but do not significantly impact extraction.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: What conflict of interest provisions under the Political Reform Act apply to a city planning director who obtains a real estate broker's license and considers working as an independent contractor for a real estate office, potentially in either Redondo Beach or Big Bear?

CONCLUSION: As planning director, Mr. Curwick must disqualify himself from making, participating in, or attempting to influence governmental decisions that will foreseeably have a material financial effect on his real estate agency, clients, or other sources of income aggregating $250 or more. If he limits his business to Big Bear rather than Redondo Beach, this will reduce the number of decisions requiring disqualification, and he can seek FPPC advice on a decision-by-decision basis if uncertain.

embedding.summary: FPPC Advice Letter A-83-238 addresses conflict of interest obligations for a Redondo Beach Planning Director who became a licensed real estate broker. The letter explains that he must disqualify himself from decisions affecting his real estate business and clients under Government Code Section 87100.
